member.name: Sarah Champion

addresses: Unit 35, Moorgate Crofts Business Centre, South Grove, Rotherham, S60 2DH

member.synopsis: Sarah Champion is the Labour MP for Rotherham , and has been an MP continually since 29 November 2012.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Injuries Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many applications to the Criminal Injuries Compensation Scheme are pending a determination by the Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the honourable Member to the answer I gave on 18 June 2026 to Question 8809: https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-10/8809.Figures up to 31 March 2025 are available in the Criminal Injuries Compensation Author...

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Injuries Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the average time taken is to conclude an application to the Criminal Injuries Compensation Scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the honourable Member to the answer I gave on 18 June 2026 to Question 8809: https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-10/8809.Figures up to 31 March 2025 are available in the Criminal Injuries Compensation Author...

writtenQuestions.heading: Nitrous Oxide: Sales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what plans her Department has to further regulate the sale of nitrous oxide cannisters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since November 2023, nitrous oxide has been controlled as a Class C drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971. Under the Act, it is an offence to produce, supply, offer to supply, possess, possess with intent to supply, import or export nitrous oxide where ...

writtenQuestions.heading: International Science Partnerships Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what is the a) total funding and b) contribution from Official Development Assistance for the International Science Partnerships Fund in the financial years 2026-27, 2027-28 and 2028-29
writtenQuestions.answerText: The International Science Partnerships Fund has an Official Development Assistance allocation of £93 million in Financial Year 26/27, and £64 million in each of Financial Years 27/28, 28/29 and 29/30, totalling £285 million over this Spending Review perio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Beavers: Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of the establishment of a dedicated Beaver Mitigation Fund to support farmers in managing localised impacts such as flooding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Support for living alongside beavers is provided through a rigorous risk assessment in the wild release licence application process and the existing management framework laid out in the 5-step beaver management approach. This is be backed up by support th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Development Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she will publish ODA allocations by country and thematic area.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As per the Minister for Development's letter of 1 June to the International Development Committee, we remain committed to publishing the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) country Official Development Assistance allocations in, or befor...

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Prison and Probation Service: Vetting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what was the average time taken to complete HM Prisons and Probation Service staff security vetting for each quarter of the last 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Within HM Prisons and Probation Service (HMPPS), security vetting is carried out to ensure individuals who wish to take up employment within the organisation can uphold the security and integrity standards expected of their role and ensure prisons and pro...

writtenQuestions.heading: Food Supply
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of agroecology on (a) food security and (b) food prices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra’s assessment of evidence on agroecological practices, including organic and other environmentally friendly systems, indicates that these approaches can enhance biodiversity and soil and biomass carbon at farm level. However, there are further profit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Countryside Stewardship Scheme: Rivers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will introduce a mechanism to incentivise the creation of river habitat corridors across neighbouring farms to the Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Farmers and land managers can already work together to create river habitat corridors across neighbouring farms through Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier. Farmers can consider Feasibility Studies to support this. The scheme already includes actions supp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Health: Developing Countries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the Official Development Assistance funding will be provided to the UK-Public Health Rapid Support Team project in the financial years 2026-27, 2027-28 and 2028-29.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The following table shows the amount of funding the UK Public Health Rapid Support Team is due to receive in Official Development Assistance funding from the Department:Financial YearTotal2026/27£8,000,0002027/28£8,000,0002028/29£8,000,000Total£24,000,000...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Slavery
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether the Government conducted an impact assessment prior to introducing changes to the Modern Slavery statutory guidance on the 11th May 2026, pertaining to the change in credibility guidance and t
writtenQuestions.answerText: Policies are regularly kept under review, and impacts are assessed as part of any proposed changes. An Equality Impact Assessment was completed as part of this process.

writtenQuestions.heading: Beavers: Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to make long-term funding available to farmers to (a) support them to make space for beavers and (b) improve land-use through beaver presence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This is a devolved matter, and the information provided therefore relates to England only. Defra recognises the benefits beavers can bring to the environment and is committed to supporting farmers and land managers to live alongside them. Defra is working...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided by the Foreign Secretary to the Member for Tooting at the last session of departmental oral questions on 21 April.

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorways: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what is the current average distance between dedicated emergency refuge areas, excluding slip roads and junctions, on All Lane Running Smart Motorways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My previous answer on 27 April 2026 set out that the average distance between places to stop in an emergency is now less than a mile (around 0.9 miles). Design standard GD301 sets out the new spacing standard (around 3/4 mile where feasible and 1 mile max...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorways: Safety Measures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what is the current average distance between emergency refuge areas on All Lane Running Smart Motorways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Highways has completed construction of 151 additional emergency areas across the All Lane Running smart motorway network, through the National Emergency Area Retrofit programme. Across every All Lane Running motorway, the average distance between...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigs: Slaughterhouses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether the planned consultation on banning the use of high-concentration carbon dioxide gas stunning for pigs will include a proposed timetable for phasing out that method.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Government’s animal welfare strategy, Defra will consult on banning carbon dioxide gas stunning of pigs and on possible timescales for phasing out this method. Further details will be set out later this year.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorways: Safety

writtenQuestions.answerText: National Highways has completed construction of 151 additional emergency areas across the all lane running (ALR) smart motorway network through the National Emergency Area Retrofit (NEAR) programme. National Highways is evaluating the effectiveness of the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ethiopia: Sudan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what discussions she has had with her Ethiopian counterpart on evidence published by the Yale School of Public Health’s Humanitarian Research Lab on the use of Ethiopian Nati
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK continues to monitor the evolving situation between Ethiopia and Sudan closely. Any further escalation would risk destabilising the region and carries a high risk of human rights abuses and atrocities. The Foreign Secretary discussed the conflict w...

writtenQuestions.heading: Slavery
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps their department will take to ensure all police forces will be kept updated to latest trends and best practice relating to modern slavery and human trafficking now support through the Moder
writtenQuestions.answerText: Modern slavery crimes are complex to investigate and prosecute, and ensuring a consistent and coordinated national policing response to modern slavery remains a priority for the Home Office.The Department worked closely with the Modern Slavery and Organis...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Chevening Scholarships Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many Chevening Scholars have subsequently claimed asylum in the UK.

registeredInterests.category: 3. Gifts, benefits and hospitality from UK sources

items.text: Name of donor: Silverstone Address of donor: The Jimmy Brown Centre, Silverstone Circuit, Towcester NN12 8TN Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Two tickets and hospitality for myself and my partner to the British Grand Prix Saturday qualifier, value £1,400 Date received: 5 July 2025 Date accepted: 5 July 2025 Donor status: company, registration 00882843 (Registered 9 July 2025)

Name of donor: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the State of Qatar Address of donor: Embassy of the State of Qatar in London, 1 South Audley Street, London W1U 4FB Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£3200), hotel (approx. £1400), local transportation (approx £500) and food (approx. £600), value £5,700 Destination of visit: Qatar Dates of visit: 19 May 2025 to 23 May 2025 Purpose of visit: Attending the Bloomberg Qatar Economic Forum 2025 and to meet with Ministers and associated organisations to discuss Qatar’s leading role in peace negotiations and humanitarian issues globally. (Registered 3 June 2025)

Name of donor: (1) Government of Jersey (2) States of Guernsey Address of donor: (1) 2 Queen Annes Gate Buildings, Dartmouth Street SW1H 9BP (2) Sir Charles Frossard House, La Charotterie, Guernsey GY1 1FH Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): (1) Flights (£210), hotel (approx. £740), local transportation (approx £750) and food (approx. £700), value £2,400 (2) Flights (£435), local transportation (approx £480) and food (approx. £116), value £1,031 Destination of visit: Jersey and Guernsey Dates of visit: 24 June 2025 to 30 June 2025 Purpose of visit: Orientation and research trip as Chair of APPG Channel Islands, with Senior Researcher (Registered 8 July 2025)

Name of donor: Doha Forum Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the state of Qatar Address of donor: Al Mirqab Tower, West Bay, Doha Qatar Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£5233) and accommodation (£405), value £5,638 Destination of visit: Qatar (Doha) Dates of visit: 5 December 2025 to 8 December 2025 Purpose of visit: To attend the Doha Forum on Diplomacy, Dialogue and Diversity and meetings with Qatari ministers to discuss Conflict Resolution. (Registered 17 December 2025)

Name of donor: Cayman Government Address of donor: c/o CICO-UK, 34 Dover Street, London W1S 4NG Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (2129.62 ), accommodation (£2303.56), transport (£199.50), meals (£899.92). I was accompanied by my partner who paid his own costs but joined me at formal dinners - food (£543.92), value £6,076.52 Destination of visit: Cayman Islands Dates of visit: 12 February 2026 to 21 February 2026 Purpose of visit: I am Chair of the APPG Cayman and led a delegation to meet with Ministers, officials and visit environmental projects and sites. (Registered 26 May 2026)
